name: Atopic Dermatitis
summary: Atopic dermatitis (eczema) is a chronic inflammatory skin disease characterized by pruritus, erythema, and barrier dysfunction. It is a component of the atopic march (often preceding asthma and allergic rhinitis). Th2 skewing, filaggrin (FLG) deficiency, and epithelial barrier defects are central. Heritability ~75%; SNP-based h² ~0.15. Key genes include FLG (loss-of-function), IL4/IL13 (Th2), and TSLP. Allergens, irritants, climate, and stress trigger flares; microbiome and diet may modify risk.
adolescent_relevance: Often begins in infancy/childhood; can persist or onset in adolescence. Psychosocial burden and transition to adult care are relevant.

top_loci.evidence: Filaggrin; barrier protein; null alleles strongly increase risk

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: FLG status and PRS inform severity; transferability across ancestries variable.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: European-dominated; FLG effects vary by ancestry.
population_equity.transferability_notes: FLG and IL13 replicate; PRS performance in non-European populations limited.
population_equity.data_gaps: Multi-ancestry GWAS; G×E for allergens and microbiome.

references.title: Multi-ancestry genome-wide association study of 21,000 cases and 95,000 controls identifies new risk loci for atopic dermatitis
Environmental and lifestyle factors in atopic dermatitis
